content.full_text:
CalifOrnia
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornrnission
September 4, L99O
Sarah Reynoso
Deputy City Attorney
office of the city AttorneY
Martin Luther Kj-ng, Jr.
civic center auilding
2180 I'{ilvia Street
BerkeLey, CA 947 04
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our FiIe No. I-90-516
Dear Ms. Reynoso:
this is in response to your letter requesting advice regard-
ing use of surptus iunds under the Political Reform Act (the
IAct").r 
since your request was for general advice, this letter
is in the nature of inf6rrnal assistance.2 (Regulation
18329 (c) (2) , copy enclosed. )
OUESTIONS
1. under the prelirninary injunction issued by the court in
service Emplovees International Union' AFL-cIo, cl,c. et aI. v.
rppq case No. clvs 89-0433 LKK-JFM, U.S. District Court, Eastern
oGtrict of California (rtsErutr) , are there any conditions under
which a candidate cannot use surplus campaign funds?
2. If the injunction is upheld, what action, if any, will
the Commission take against candidates who have transferred
surplus funds to current carnpaigns?
1^ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. A1I statutory references
are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Cornrnission
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations section
18000, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 
2
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 InforrnaL assistance does not
innunity provided by an opinion
83114; Regulation 18329 (c) (3). )
provide the requestor with the
or formal- written advice. (Section
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4.O8O7 . (916\322-5660


Fi■e NO. I-90-5■6
Page 2
CONCLUSIONS
1. Surplus campaign funds and use thereof are not addressed
in the injunction issued in the EELp case.
2. Actions taken by candidates pursuant to a 1awfu1 court
order allowing such actions witl not be subject to any enforcement
action by the Commission.
FACTS
You represent several elected officers who have indicated
that they require g'uidance with respect to the use of rrsurplusrl
funds which rnay remain after their November 1990 reelectj.on
contests. your letter does not recite specific facts rrhj'ch have
given rise to your question, but instead linits itself to the two
questions posed above.
DI SCUSS ION
The SEIU case challenges the constitutionality 
of certain
provisions of Proposition 73. Specifically, . plaintiffs 
are.chal-
ienging the fiscal year provisions in Proposition 73, the Lirnita-
tions on use of contributions for election to one specific office,
and the restrictions on use of canpaign funds raised prior to the
effective date of Proposition 73.
A prelirninary injunction issued on May 15, 1989 enjoined,
inter alia, enforcement of the ban on transferrj.ng carnpaign funds
between a candidate's own cornmittees per the language of Section
85202 (b) and Section 85304.
To date, the SEIU injunction remains in effect. 
Trial in the
SEIU case was hel,d in February, 1990, with closing arguments heard
in Apri1, 1990. Parties are presently awaiting the court's rul,-
ing.
As it now stands, a candidate is free to transfer canpaign
funds from one of his/her committees to another of his/her con-
mittees. Thus a city councilman who subsequently ran for mayor
could transfer funds from his council cornmittee to his mayoral
committee without concern until the funds become trsurplustr under
the Act. see clark Advice Letter, No. A-90-432.
Your initial 
question focuses on use of surplus funds.
Surplus funds are regulated by Section 85807, a section not
enacted by Proposition 73, and therefore not within the purview of
the SEIU injunction.
As a threshold matter, it is inportant to note that rrsurplus
fundsrr have a very specific rneaning in the Act. In the case of an
unsuccessful candidate, surplus fundsrr refer to funds remaining
in the candidate,s account at the end of the post election reoort-
incr oeriod. Until that tine such reroaining funds are not subject


File No. I-90-516
Page 3
to the strictures of Section 85807, and may be transferred. 
In
the case of a successful candidate, nonies remaining from the
campaign do not becone rrsurplus fundsrt until the official 
leaves
office. 
At that tine, the remaining money becomes rrsurplusrr and
is subject to the disposition rules set forth in Section 85807.
Until nonies become rrsurplus funds,tr i.e. during the window
period betr,reen an unsuccessful bid for office and concLusion of
Lhe post election reporting period, or before leaving office, 
a
cand-idate may use calnpaign funds for any purpose consistent with
the Act's limitation on use of campaign funds. (section 85800-
85805.) The SEIU injunction would permit a transfer to another of
the candidate's conruittees during the above-noted window period.
once the funds become rtsurplus,rr the candidate may do onlv the
following with surplus funds.
1. pay outstanding carnpaign debts;
2. repay contributions on a pro-rata basis;
3. donate to any bona fide charitable, educationaL,
civic, religious, or sirnilar tax-exempt, nonprofit organiza-
tion, where no substantial part of the proceeds will have a
material financial effect on the former candidate or elected
officer, any menber of his or her immediate farnily or his her
campaign treasurer I
4. contribute to a political party or committee so long
as the funds are not used to make contributions in support of
or opposition to a candidate for elective office; and
5. contribute to support or oppose any candi,date for
federal office, any candidate for elective office in a state
other than California or any ballot measure. (Section
8s807. )
The SEIU injunction, handed down in the context of a chal-
lenge to Proposition 73, has no relevance to the use of rrsurplus
funds,rr and thus the answer to your initial 
question would have to
be negative.
Your second question, asks what action the commission would
take agai.nst candidates who have transferred surplus funds to cur-
rent campaigns if the injunction is lifted.
If you are referring to transfers of funds rernaining in a
canpaign account prior to terrnination of the post election report-
ing period or an official's 
departure from office, then there
would be no violation of any kind requiring action by the Connis-
sion. A transfer effected lawfully during the period rdhen the
injunction obtained would be an acceptable transfer. 
Holrever,
once the funds took on the character of trsurplus fundsrr per Sec-
tion 85807, any transfer of funds to a current canpaign would be a


FiIe No. I-90-516
Page 4
violation of the Act, and subject to an enforcement action if a
cornplaint were filed.
Please note that any transfer of funds between a candidate's
own committees is subject to the contribution linits of Proposi-
tion 73. Thus, if an individual contributor contributes the
maxinum allowed contribution of $1,000 to a campaign in a
particular fiscal year, the fact that the candidate transfers
those funds fron that contributor to another account does not
perruit the contributor to contribute another $1,000 to the first
account in the sane fiscal year. See, gIeIE, supra.
I trust that the above answers your questions. If you need
further information, please do not hesitate to telephone ne at
(916) 322-59OL.
Sincerely,
scott
Acting
Ha l labrin
General
レ
t
By: Susan L.
counsef ,
SH:SLB:p■h
Enc■osure
